results.title: Acceptance and Rejection Rates for Flexible Work Arrangement Requests, and Top Three Reasons for Rejecting
results.summary: The Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests (TG-FWAR) commenced on 1 December 2024 to facilitate mutually beneficial flexible work arrangements without mandating reporting to the Ministry of Manpower to avoid compliance costs. Employers must consider formal requests on reasonable business grounds, while the Government adopts an educational approach providing templates, guides, and training. Entities facing implementation challenges or employees with unconsidered requests may seek assistance from the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices. The Minister did not disclose specific acceptance or rejection rates or the top three reasons for rejections as requested.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: The debate on the Adjournment of Debate was moved by Mr Sharael Taha and subsequently adjourned. The Speaker granted consent for the debate to conclude, with the matter to be resumed tomorrow. No specific policy measures, legislative changes, or operational constraints were discussed during this procedural motion. The session ended without further action or announcements regarding government programmes or statistics.

results.title: Anti-bullying Reporting and Investigating Protocols
results.summary: The Ministry of Education will provide an oral reply regarding the publication of an annual School Safety Scorecard, the establishment of a national anonymous anti-bullying reporting line, and the development of investigation protocols for educators. The response will also address whether minimum anti-bullying policy standards will be mandated across all schools and detail collaborations with external agencies to strengthen trauma-informed practices. Specific timelines, eligibility criteria, and substantive answers to questions about substantiation percentages, suspension numbers, and the involvement of child safety experts have not yet been stated. The Minister indicated that these details will be presented at the next available opportunity alongside other related parliamentary questions.

results.title: Allegation that Cyber Attacks against Information Systems of Ninth Asian Winter Games in Harbin Originated from Singapore
results.summary: The Government states it has no evidence that the 40,000 cyberattacks reported by China against the Ninth Asian Winter Games in Harbin originated from Singapore. While acknowledging that malicious actors can mask their true origins, the Minister confirmed Singapore does not permit the use of its digital infrastructure for such activities. The Cyber Security Agency has engaged its Chinese counterpart to investigate any shared information regarding these alleged attacks. No further action or specific timeline was announced pending receipt of additional data from the People's Republic of China.

results.title: Adjustments to Vehicular Emissions Scheme and EV Early Adoption Incentive Beyond 2027
results.summary: The Electric Vehicle Early Adoption Incentive will cease on 1 January 2027, while the Vehicular Emissions Scheme extends until the end of 2027. Future reviews of the scheme will consider EV adoption rates and upfront cost differences across powertrains. Members are directed to the Acting Minister for Transport's response to the Adjournment Motion on 22 September 2025 for further context regarding these adjustments and their impact on the COE market. Specific details on post-2027 adjustments beyond the stated extension dates remain under consideration.

results.title: Addressing Issue of Pigeon Roosting and Defecation at Air-conditioner Ledges of HDB Flats
results.summary: National Parks Board (NParks) manages pigeon populations through a science-based approach involving population control, habitat modification, and enforcement against illegal feeding. Since July 2024, a pilot program in Ang Mo Kio, Bishan Toa-Payoh, and Tanjong Pagar reduced the estimated pigeon population by approximately 50% and lowered related feedback by 34% compared to control areas. The plan expanded to Jalan Besar, Marsiling-Yew Tee, and Nee Soon in June 2025, with phased scaling across Singapore. While NParks and HDB are studying deterrent measures like bird nettings, there are currently no plans to modify the Home Improvement Programme or upgrade clothes drying rack designs to address pigeon roosting at air-conditioner ledges. Residents are urged not to feed birds and to maintain a clean environment to support collective management efforts.

results.title: Assessing Graduate Employability and Strengthening Student-industry Collaboration Beyond Internships
results.summary: The Government monitors graduate employability via the MOE Graduate Employment Survey and MOM Labour Force Survey, noting stable six-month employment rates and median salary increases of 3% to 5% in 2024. While long-term unemployment for workers under 30 remains low (0.6% to 1.2%), IHLs are aligning curricula with evolving industry demands, including AI integration, through regular reviews with sector agencies and industry partners. Beyond mandatory internships, polytechnics and ITEs utilize industry attachments, while universities offer similar opportunities for most undergraduates. Initiatives include interdisciplinary courses, Centres of Innovation projects, and the development of critical thinking and digital literacy competencies to ensure resilience against technological shifts. MOE and IHLs will continue collaborating with industry to deepen applied learning and adjust programmes to current economic conditions.

results.title: Assessment of Effectiveness of Parental Education Programmes on Healthy Screentime Habits for Children
results.summary: The Government launched Grow Well SG in January to support parents in managing children's screen use through practical tips, health checklists, and community talks, with resources available via multiple digital and physical channels. While these measures are currently being monitored for effectiveness, there are no plans to mandate parental education programmes for new parents due to varying family needs. However, all Singaporean parents of newborns in 2025 will receive a parenting journal with a guide recommending no screen use for infants as part of the SG60 Baby Gift Pack, with similar guides planned for subsequent years. The Government will continue to engage parents to address challenges like using devices as "digital babysitters" and strengthen tools to foster healthier screen time habits in the digital age.

results.title: Assessment of Service Quality Levels for Telephone Calls made to IMH's Main and Outpatient Appointment Lines
results.summary: IMH conducts monthly service quality assessments for its Main and Outpatient Appointment Lines. Recent increases in abandonment rates and average handling times are attributed to rising call volumes and a major IT system transition. To address these issues, IMH has implemented measures including redeploying manpower and improving operational processes. Additionally, caller satisfaction data will be tracked starting from late-2025 via post-call surveys. The Minister noted that these metrics are part of ongoing service quality monitoring efforts.

results.title: Avoiding "Long Weekends" when Scheduling Polling Day for General Elections
results.summary: The Minister stated the Government cannot commit to avoiding polling days during long weekends, noting election dates depend on situational exigencies. Regarding the 2025 General Election, 99,140 non-voters restored their names by 1 September 2025, exceeding half of the total non-voters. Approximately 97% of these restorations were completed via Singpass, while the remainder occurred in person at community centres, ServiceSG Centres, or the Elections Department. The Minister encouraged remaining non-voters to restore their names early to ensure participation in the next election. The specific count of voters restoring names via appointment at the Elections Department office was not separately disclosed in the response.

results.title: Breakdown of Property Types Where Fires Related to Power-assisted Personal Mobility Devices Occurred
results.summary: Minister K Shanmugam provided a table detailing the number of fires involving power-assisted personal mobility devices across six property categories for the last five years. The data covers owner-occupied and public rental HDB flats, condominiums, landed houses, non-residential commercial buildings, and non-building locations like roads. Specific figures for each year and property type are contained within the referenced table. No further analysis or future projections regarding these fire incidents were stated in the response.

results.title: Cause and Actions Taken for Sinkhole Formation at Tanjong Katong Road South in July 2025
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority (BCA) is conducting an independent investigation under the Building Control Act into the sinkhole at Tanjong Katong Road South that formed on 26 July 2025, while the Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment (MSE) runs a parallel internal inquiry. The incident occurred when a contractor's 16-metre-deep sewer shaft, constructed with reinforced concrete caisson rings, experienced structural failure, causing soil to flow into the shaft and creating the sinkhole adjacent to the worksite. Following the event, PUB, in consultation with BCA and the Land Transport Authority, performed safety assessments on nearby roads which showed no abnormalities, leading to the progressive reopening of affected sections starting 2 August 2025. As investigations remain ongoing due to the incident's complexity, no conclusions regarding the specific cause or penalties have been released, and the full findings are expected within several months.

results.title: Causes for Increase in Outrage of Modesty Cases in 1H2025 and Longer-term Trends of Voyeurism Identified
results.summary: In the first half of 2025, outrage of modesty cases rose 21.2% year-on-year, with over half involving offenders known to victims, while voyeurism cases decreased 2.3%. The Ministry identified public transport networks as a primary concern for cases involving strangers and has deployed specific countermeasures, including training school and community counsellors to intervene early regarding inappropriate sexual behaviours among youths. Additionally, the Police collaborate with transport operators to issue crime advisories and support the "Riders-on-Watch" interest group, which currently comprises over 14,000 commuters acting as community eyes and ears. The Ministry emphasizes that public vigilance and immediate reporting of suspicious behaviours to staff or the Police remain crucial for preventing, detecting, and deterring these crimes.

results.title: Comparing 2024 and 2025 Graduate Employment Rates and Median Salaries by Course Cluster
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower reported that the 2025 graduate cohort achieved a 44% permanent employment rate by June, an improvement over the 37% rate for the 2024 cohort. However, specific data comparing employment rates and median gross monthly salaries by course cluster for the 2025 cohort is not yet available. These detailed metrics will be collected during the Graduate Employment Survey scheduled for November 2025. Consequently, a direct comparison of salary figures and cluster-specific employment rates between the two cohorts cannot currently be provided.

results.title: Complaints Received against Pet Breeders and Ensuring Pet Shops Procure Pets from Licensed Breeders
results.summary: Over the past five years, the National Parks Board received approximately 100 annual complaints against both licensed and unlicensed pet breeders. Under Animal and Veterinary Service licensing requirements, pet shops are obligated to procure cats and dogs exclusively from licensed pet breeders. This measure aims to regulate the pet trade by ensuring supply chain compliance with licensing standards. The Minister confirmed these operational constraints apply specifically to feline and canine procurement within licensed retail environments.

results.title: Contractual Obligations for Hawkers in Social Enterprise Hawker Centres to Provide Free Meals
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding NEA's permission for Social Enterprise Hawker Centre operators to mandate free meals in contracts will not be answered now. Instead, this inquiry, along with similar Parliamentary Questions, will be addressed at the next Sitting on 24 September 2025. The response will be provided then to offer a more holistic reply covering the appropriateness of such contractual practices in future agreements. No immediate guidance or policy change was confirmed during this session.

results.title: Cross-agency Sharing of Data to Prompt Follow-up by Child Protection Services on Potential Child Abuse or Neglect Cases
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) maintains customised cross-agency processes with schools, hospitals, and law enforcement to identify potential child abuse or neglect cases. Schools utilise the Education Sector-Specific Screening Guide and conduct home visits for chronic absenteeism, as this factor alone does not automatically trigger intervention. Hospitals lodge police reports and alert medical social workers for non-accidental injuries, while law enforcement refers cases to the National Anti-Violence and Sexual Harassment Helpline (NAVH) when family arrests leave children without caregivers or raise abuse concerns. MSF currently shares data with various agencies and will continually assess whether automated data alerts and triggers can provide a more effective response, implementing them where feasible.

results.title: Data on Enrolment, Graduation, Non-completion and Employment of Students under ITE Work-Study Diploma Programme
results.summary: The Minister for Education reported that ITE Work-Study Diploma enrolment grew from 100 in 2018 to approximately 1,300 in 2024, with completion rates stabilising around 80%. Surveys of graduates six months post-completion indicate consistent full-time employment across sectors at roughly 90%, with median monthly salaries near $2,900 comparable to Polytechnic diploma holders. Approximately 70% of graduates remain with their training employers, while about 10% progress directly to university degree or polytechnic post-diploma programmes. For non-completers, ITE provides career counselling and allows re-application to subsequent intakes, where previously completed modules are recognised for related courses or work-based learning.

results.title: Data on Fresh Graduates Employed on Fixed-term Contracts with Durations of One Year or Less
results.summary: The Minister for Education clarified that the Graduate Employment Survey tracks temporary employment, defined as casual or fixed-term contracts under one year, while contracts of one year or more are classified as permanent. Over the past five years, an average of 90% of employed graduates held full-time permanent roles, with only 10% in temporary positions. Temporary employment rates varied by institution, standing at approximately 5% for autonomous universities, 20% for polytechnics, 10% for ITE, and 20% for private institutions. The Minister noted that close to half of polytechnic graduates in temporary roles cited further studies as their reason for choosing such work. Data on fixed-term contracts of one year or less are not separately tracked as distinct from permanent employment.

results.title: Data on Scam Losses, Scam Typology, Median Age of Victims and Time Taken for Freezing of Funds Following Restriction Orders
results.summary: The Minister directed members to consult half-yearly Scam and Cybercrime Briefs on the Police website for quarterly scam loss data by typology and victim age distribution. For the first half of this year, the median age of victims was 43 years, with over $56.7 million recovered. Regarding fund freezing timelines, the Police do not track the specific duration after restriction orders are issued; however, the typical timeframe is within hours or a day. Data on recovery proportions and specific quarterly loss amounts are available via the published briefs.

results.title: Data on Successful Applications for Public Rental Flats Made By Individuals Undergoing Divorce and Separation Proceedings
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, the Housing and Development Board (HDB) approved approximately 6,100 public rental flat requests from individuals who had completed divorce or separation proceedings or were undergoing proceedings with ancillary matters settled. An additional 186 requests were approved on a case-by-case basis without settled ancillary matters, bringing the annual total to roughly 1,200 to 1,300 approvals. HDB does not differentiate between applicants based on whether proceedings are completed or ongoing with settled matters, nor does it track applications by documentation type. Consequently, the Minister could not provide the specific breakdown by interim judgment, deed of separation, or case-by-case basis requested by the Member.

results.title: Detection of Illegal Purchases of Nicotine Pouches by Singaporeans
results.summary: The Health Sciences Authority (HSA) reports no detected cases of illegal nicotine pouch or snus purchases from the SnusAsia website to date. HSA collaborates with the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) to block the site for breaching the Tobacco (Control of Advertisement and Sales) Act and works with local platforms to remove listings. The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority seizes prohibited tobacco products at checkpoints. These joint efforts monitor online sales and control access to nicotine pouches, particularly addressing concerns regarding youth usage through enforcement actions against illegal imports and advertisements.

results.title: Duties and Privileges of the Leader of the Opposition
results.summary: Following the 3 May 2025 General Election, Prime Minister Lawrence Wong confirmed Mr Pritam Singh will continue as Leader of the Opposition in the 15th Parliament with unchanged duties and privileges from the 14th Parliament. Parliamentary arrangements include a seat directly across the Prime Minister, first right of response to question Ministers, and a 40-minute debate speech time versus the standard 20 minutes for other MPs. The Leader receives an allocated office, staff support, resources, and a Parliamentary allowance double that of other Members. Duties involve presenting alternative views, scrutinising government actions, nominating opposition Members for Select Committees, and attending official state functions. The Leader may also receive confidential government briefings on national issues like security and external relations, particularly during crises, to foster cross-party understanding. A Motion regarding the speech time adjustment will be moved subsequently.

results.title: Causes for Recent MRT and LRT Breakdowns, and Measures to Improve Rail Reliability and Review Financial Penalties
results.summary: Recent MRT and LRT breakdowns on 3, 19 July and 12, 15 August 2025 have prompted a review of reliability metrics, specifically the Mean Kilometres Between Failure (MKBF) target of 1 million train-km. While the Cross Island Line (CIL) remains the ultimate solution for NEL congestion, LTA will augment bus capacity along the corridor rather than treating buses as a complete substitute due to capacity constraints. The Land Transport Authority will work with unions and operators to support worker training and upgrading under the New Rail Financing Framework, acknowledging that operators retain responsibility for maintenance. LTA will also address specific connectivity gaps at Jalan Kayu (Fernvale) by providing marginal bus support, though full resiliency measures await further funding for infrastructure upgrades and engineering time required to manage expanding network complexity.

results.title: Efficacy of Career Coaching at Autonomous Universities in Supporting Graduate Employment Outcomes
results.summary: Autonomous Universities (AUs) offer Education and Career Guidance services including career coaching, talks, and job search skill training to support students and fresh graduates. AUs track programme performance using internal surveys and student feedback rather than Ministry-collected data, as the Ministry does not gather statistics on specific coaching sessions. The Minister stated that it is difficult to directly attribute improved employment outcomes to any single intervention. Consequently, no specific percentage of students receiving coaching or definitive efficacy metrics are provided in the official response.

results.title: Election of Deputy Speakers
results.summary: Mr Zaqy Mohamad proposed the election of Mr Christopher de Souza and Mr Xie Yao Quan as Deputy Speakers of Parliament. Ms Yeo Wan Ling supported the proposal, citing the candidates' commitment to good governance, impartiality, and the promotion of constructive, respectful debate across party lines. The motion was put forward to fill the Deputy Speaker positions, with both nominees expected to bring sound judgement and a strong sense of duty to their roles. No specific timeline or additional eligibility conditions were stated in the transcript regarding this election.

results.title: Electric Vehicle Charging Rates at HDB and Private Car Parks, and Impact of Mandating Dual-operators
results.summary: The median EV charging price is $0.66/kWh at HDB carparks versus $0.74/kWh at private commercial carparks, reflecting commercial decisions by operators. In HDB tenders, the evaluation framework weights quality more than price, and operators hold no exclusive rights to operate. The Ministry has not mandated dual-operators, though the Land Transport Authority may call future tenders to install additional chargers. Charging prices vary based on operator, location, and charger power rating.

results.title: Electrical Safety Checks for Flats Undergoing Home Improvement and Enhancement for Active Seniors Programmes
results.summary: Since 2021, the Housing and Development Board (HDB) has conducted mandatory safety checks on Residual Current Circuit Breakers (RCCBs) and water heater installations for all flats undergoing the Home Improvement Programme (HIP), replacing any faulty components at full HDB cost. For the Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE) programme, HDB performs similar checks on RCCBs and wiring when residents apply to replace light switches with rocker switches, covering replacement costs for faults while advising owners to rectify non-compliant lighting installations themselves before switch replacement. In 2024, approximately 5% of HIP flats had faulty RCCBs and 28% had non-compliant water heater installations, whereas less than 1% of EASE applicants faced faulty RCCBs; all identified faults have been rectified. HDB does not track the specific number of EASE flats with non-compliant lighting installations.

results.title: Eligibility and Appeal Avenues for Merchants to Participate in CDC Voucher Scheme
results.summary: The CDC Voucher Scheme primarily targets hawkers and heartland merchants in HDB areas, though non-HDB premises are considered on a case-by-case basis where few local merchants exist. While 2023 expanded voucher usage to supermarkets, only half of the 2024 tranche applies there, with the other half reserved for heartland merchants to balance resident needs and business support. As of the latest data, over 23,000 merchants are onboarded, achieving a 97% spend rate in 2024 and 98% resident satisfaction regarding merchant availability in January 2025. The Acting Minister confirmed that specific eligibility criteria for non-HDB merchants and the communication of appeal grounds were not detailed in the response, noting instead that CDCs continue collaborating with partners to onboard eligible heartland businesses.

results.title: Employer-employee Relationship in GRaduate Industry Traineeships and Mid-Career Pathways Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower deferred answering the question regarding the rationale for not classifying GRIT and Mid-Career Pathways Programme trainees as employees of their host organisations. He directed the Member to refer to a reply scheduled for 23 September concerning graduate employment and the GRIT programme. No specific policy details, eligibility criteria, or operational constraints were provided in this exchange. The substantive explanation for the employment status distinction remains pending the release of the official response on the specified date.

results.title: Employers' Responsibilities and Costs in Repatriating Migrant Domestic Workers
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower confirmed that employers of work permit holders, including Migrant Domestic Workers (MDWs), must bear the full cost of repatriation upon termination of employment. This requirement ensures MDWs are not left stranded in Singapore due to disputes over shared responsibilities. Employers are informed of these obligations before their workers enter Singapore and during the mandatory Employers' Orientation Programme for first-time employers. The Ministry will not review this specific cost-sharing arrangement to prevent situations where workers lack the means to return home.

results.title: Enhancing Border Controls and Extending Extra-territorial Provisions in Misuse of Drugs Act to Deter Smuggling of Vapes
results.summary: Between 15 September 2024 and 14 September 2025, authorities intercepted 76 large-scale smuggling attempts and 57 via postal channels, seizing over 500,000 e-vaporisers and more than 3,000 components. The Misuse of Drugs Act's extra-territorial provisions under section 8A apply to Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents abusing etomidate overseas, now listed as a Class C controlled drug since 1 September. Enforcement agencies, including the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority, Singapore Police Force, and Central Narcotics Bureau, are conducting inland operations and deploying officers at the upcoming RTS Link to address cross-border trafficking risks. A new legislation under the Ministry of Health is expected next year to provide faster legal powers for classifying new substances and mandating rehabilitation without waiting for comprehensive clinical data.

results.title: Evaluation Criteria for Partner Operator Applications for Preschool Services
results.summary: For the current Partner Operator scheme term, ECDA evaluated applications based on track records in quality childcare, financial sustainability, centre accessibility, and local demand. Applicants also needed to demonstrate commitment to affordable services and enhancing professional development for early childhood professionals. Unsuccessful applicants received feedback indicating ECDA considered their relative suitability against these specific factors, though detailed written feedback outlining exact shortcomings was not provided. ECDA is now evaluating applications for the next term commencing 1 January 2026, with results to be announced soon. Selection principles and communication practices for the upcoming term will broadly align with current practices.

results.title: Excessive Price Pass-through to Essential Goods from Global Supply Chain Disruptions and Rising Rentals
results.summary: To mitigate global supply chain disruptions and rising costs, the Government is diversifying import sources via Free Trade Agreements and enforcing anti-competitive practices. Budget 2025 introduced support measures including $800 in Community Development Council vouchers for all households and additional U-Save rebates for eligible HDB residents. One-off SG60 commemorative vouchers of $600 for adults and $800 for seniors provide further relief. Since 1 September, major supermarkets NTUC FairPrice, Sheng Siong, and Prime Supermarket have piloted unit price displays for selected groceries, with Cold Storage and Giant joining on 31 October to aid price comparison. Operators also maintain affordable options through campaigns like FairPrice's Everyday Low Price, CHAS discounts, and SG60-specific reductions on essential goods.

results.title: Exclusion of Businesses from CDC Voucher Scheme and Appeal Outcomes
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that the CDC Voucher Scheme primarily supports heartland merchants in residential areas but includes non-HDB premises on a case-by-case basis where essential services are needed. To date, over 23,000 hawkers and merchants, including those with successful appeals, have been onboarded. The Ministry does not track the specific number of ineligible businesses in non-HDB premises providing essential community services. CDCs will continue collaborating with partners like the Federation of Merchants' Association to onboard eligible merchants in heartlands.

results.title: Exempted Business
results.summary: Ms Indranee Rajah moved a motion to extend parliamentary proceedings beyond the automatic 7.00 pm termination time. This procedural request seeks the Speaker's consent and general Member assent to continue business set down on the Order Paper. The debate focuses solely on the administrative mechanism for extending sitting hours rather than specific policy measures, legislation, or financial data. No substantive policy signals, eligibility rules, or operational constraints regarding exempted businesses were addressed in this segment of the transcript.

results.title: Expanding CDC Voucher Eligibility to Shophouse Merchants and Considerations for Appeals
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth explained that the CDC Voucher Scheme, launched in June 2020, primarily targets hawkers and merchants in HDB heartlands, including those in National Environment Agency-managed centres. While over 23,000 merchants have been onboarded to date, inclusion of privately-owned shophouse merchants outside these designated areas occurs only on a case-by-case basis where few or no heartland merchants exist nearby. The Minister did not specify the exact number of additional eligible merchants if all privately-owned shophouses were included, nor did he detail the specific factors considered for resident appeals regarding these locations. Consequently, the precise eligibility criteria for such appeals and the projected count of additional merchants remain unstated in the response.

results.title: Expanding Pilot on Displaying of Unit Prices in Supermarkets to Permanent Implementation
results.summary: The Government will launch a pilot on displaying unit prices in supermarkets starting 1 September 2025, in collaboration with major operators and the Consumers Association of Singapore. The initiative aims to assess consumer feedback on price comparison effectiveness and evaluate operational feasibility for businesses. Insights gathered from this pilot will determine whether a broader permanent rollout delivers meaningful value to consumers and operators before any further implementation is considered.

results.title: Explaining Misinformation and Singapore Government's Positions on Polarising Overseas Conflicts
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) is enhancing public understanding of Singapore's foreign policy through dialogue sessions, workshops, and accessible social media content targeting students, youths, businesses, and the media. These engagements aim to equip community leaders with the ability to address misinformation and explain the rationale behind government positions on polarising overseas conflicts. The approach emphasises a whole-of-society effort to foster discernment among Singaporeans, ensuring they remain united against divisive rhetoric while staying vigilant about national interests. MFA will continue collaborating with community leaders to help fellow citizens understand the fundamentals guiding foreign policy decisions.

results.title: Extending Drone Operation Training to All New NS Enlistees to Maximise Operational Versatility across Vocations
results.summary: The Singapore Armed Forces plans to train all service-fit recruits, including combat-fit and non-combat-fit, to operate and counter drones following Basic Military Training. Competencies will be further honed during full-time national service at leadership schools and Army units. Additionally, drone training will be progressively rolled out for Operationally Ready National Servicemen. The first National Service units are scheduled to begin operating drones during in-camp trainings starting mid-2026. Current Full-time National Servicemen and Operationally Ready National Servicemen who have not received such training will also be upskilled through these progressive rollout measures.

results.title: Extension of Concessionary Migrant Domestic Worker Levy to First-time Expectant Parents Prior to Delivery of Child
results.summary: The Ministry for Manpower confirmed that the $60 concessionary Migrant Domestic Worker Levy applies to households with a Singapore Citizen under 16 years old or other care recipients, making expectant parents eligible upon the birth of a child rather than two to three months prior. The Minister stated the Government will continue to monitor the need for extending this concession or introducing alternative support options for expectant parents before delivery. No specific fiscal implications, beneficiary numbers, or timelines for policy reviews were provided in the response.

results.title: Extension of SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support to Workers Engaged on Contracts for Service with Non-renewals
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower confirmed that the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme (SJSS) will not be extended to workers on contracts for service with non-renewals. Instead, a related question filed by Ms Elysa Chen regarding the inclusion of such vulnerable workers will be addressed jointly in the upcoming sitting on 23 September 2025. No immediate policy change or eligibility expansion has been announced at this time.

results.title: Feasibility Study on Safe Charging Zones at HDB Void Decks for Residents Using PMAs
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority confirmed there are no commonly recognised international fire safety standards for Personal Mobility Aids, preventing the establishment of specific safe charging zones at HDB void decks. While UL2272 and EN15194 standards apply to motorised devices and power-assisted bicycles respectively, LTA will continue to monitor international standard developments and review existing guidelines on Active Mobility Device storage and charging. Concurrently, the agency is enhancing public education through the Be-Device-Safe programme, a joint initiative with the Singapore Civil Defence Force, to inform users about fire risks and safe home charging practices.

results.title: Fires Related to Power-assisted Bicycles and Mobility Aids, and Encouraging Installation of Home Fire Alarm Devices in Public Rental Flats
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, fires involving Active Mobility Devices (AMDs) in HDB estates declined from 187 to a lower count, though non-compliant devices accounted for 72% of such incidents in the last three years. The Land Transport Authority requires LTA approval for importing Power-assisted Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices since June 2021 and enforces strict safety standards. Over 80% of Public Rental Flats now have Home Fire Alarm Devices (HFADs), with the remaining 20% being progressively installed free of charge. Seniors accessing the Enhancement for Active Seniors programme receive up to 95% subsidies for HFAD installation, while existing homes undergoing fire safety alterations are also prioritized. As of June 2025, approximately 191,000 HDB flats, or 17% of the total, are equipped with HFADs. The government does not currently plan to install HFADs in corridors or centralised systems but continues rolling out

results.title: Help for Nicotine and Vape Addiction, and Extent of Manufacture and Surrender of Etomidate-laced Vapes in Singapore
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health addressed parliamentary questions regarding nicotine addiction support, etomidate-laced vape risks, and surrender incentives. Specific data on QuitLine calls and agency approaches for etomidate cessation since 1 June 2025, alongside the count of surrendered vapes containing etomidate between 25 July and 31 August 2025, were not provided in the transcript. The Minister confirmed that individuals surrendering etomidate-laced vapes from 1 September 2025 face no penalty. Details on the Government's risk assessment of manufacturing etomidate analogues, plans to phase out etomidate in medical settings, and the specific alternative anaesthetic agents were not stated. Furthermore, the number of rehabilitative programmes, the extent of community partnerships with schools and parents, and concrete plans to expand early intervention programmes for vaping diversion were not detailed in the oral reply.

results.title: Help under Grow Well SG for Children from Vulnerable Families to Benefit from Early Healthy Lifestyle Interventions
results.summary: Grow Well SG provides universal preventive health services to all children, specifically benefiting lower-income households who lack access to such guidance. Primary 1 to 3 students receive personalised Health Plans and Lifestyle Prescriptions via the Health Promotion Board's annual school screenings, with plans to expand coverage to other age groups progressively. The Ministry has partnered with the Ministry of Social and Family Development to strengthen support for ComLink+ families, nudging them to inculcate healthy habits early and access appropriate health services.

results.title: Impact of GRaduate Industry Traineeships on Graduates' Placement, Development and Earning Potential
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed Members to refer to an oral reply scheduled for 23 September 2025 regarding graduate employment and the GRaduate Industry Traineeships Programme. This response will address the determination of the $1,800 to $2,400 traineeship allowance and its comparison with average fresh graduate salaries. Additionally, the reply will provide mean and median starting salaries for SGUnited Traineeship participants versus non-participants, including those employed by host organisations or elsewhere. Specific figures for these salary comparisons were not provided in the current transcript.

results.title: Impact of Singapore's Humanitarian Aid to Gaza and Singapore's Next Steps as Conflict Escalates
results.summary: On 22 September 2025, the Ministry for Foreign Affairs addressed written questions regarding Singapore's humanitarian response to the Gaza conflict, confirming a total contribution of S$22 million since October 2023. The Minister stated that coordination with on-the-ground partners ensures equitable distribution but did not provide a specific breakdown of allocations, nor did the transcript disclose the percentage of aid delayed, denied, or obstructed. Singapore is monitoring international developments, including other nations' plans to recognise Palestine as a sovereign state at the UN General Assembly in September 2025 and potential UN Security Council resolutions for peacekeeping forces. The Government indicated it will continue to assess these factors alongside its own position on recognising Palestine and ensuring future aid tranches reach intended recipients, though specific next steps regarding escalation were not detailed.

results.title: Impact of US Tariffs on Singapore Companies and Economy, Update on Negotiations and Support for Key Sectors
results.summary: The US baseline tariff of 10% took effect on 9 April 2025, coinciding with a 28% year-on-year decline in Singapore's exports to the US between April and August 2025. Despite this, Singapore's economy grew 4.3% in the first half of 2025, with 2025 GDP growth forecast between 1.5% and 2.5%, though moderation is expected in the second half as front-loading effects dissipate. The Ministry of Trade and Industry monitors potential additional section 232 tariffs on sectors like pharmaceuticals and semiconductors. To support businesses, the Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce launched the Business Adaptation Grant for supply chain optimisation over two years and the GRaduate Industry Traineeships programme to aid fresh graduates. Further support for key sectors remains under assessment.

results.title: Implementation Timeline and Order of Roll-out for Two-carriage Trains for Sengkang-Punggol LRT
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the 25 new two-carriage Light Rail Vehicles for the Sengkang-Punggol LRT will be fully deployed by end-2028. Two units are already in service, with the remaining 23 to be rolled out sequentially based on operational requirements and prevailing passenger loading conditions. The specific order of rollout across the Sengkang and Punggol loops is determined by these real-time factors rather than a fixed schedule. No further details on the detailed implementation timeline for each loop were provided in the response.

results.title: Implication of Renaming of US Department of Defense to Department of War on Singapore’s Foreign Policy, Defence Strategy and Regional Stability
results.summary: The renaming of the US Department of Defense to the Department of War is an internal US administrative decision with no stated impact on Singapore's foreign policy or defence strategy. During a September 2025 visit, Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence Chan Chun Sing reaffirmed Singapore's excellent bilateral defence relationship with the US under Secretary of War Pete Hegseth. Both nations discussed deepening cooperation, and Singapore continues to support US engagement in the Asia-Pacific, consistent with the 1990 Memorandum of Understanding renewed in 2019 by President Donald Trump and then-Prime Minister Lee Hsien Loong. No changes to regional stability or operational constraints were indicated.

results.title: Improving OneService App to Include Reporting of Vaping, Non-Singpass Verification and Automated Translation
results.summary: The Minister stated that existing vaping reporting channels are currently adequate, with the Health Sciences Authority extending hotline hours to seven days a week since July 2025 and launching an online form. While a dedicated OneService App pathway for vaping was not added, the Municipal Services Office offers an AI-enabled chatbot on WhatsApp, Telegram, and Instagram that complements the app without requiring Singpass authentication. This alternative verification channel is accessible to youths and seniors and does not mandate Singpass. The Ministry is also studying enhancements to allow the chatbot to process feedback in vernacular languages to improve reporting uptake.

results.title: Inclusion of Expected Child in Calculation of Per Capita Income for Determining Rental Rates under Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the Parenthood Provisional Housing Scheme (PPHS) does not differentiate rental rates based on per capita income. A monthly household income ceiling of $7,000 was implemented in August 2021 to target support for families less able to afford open market rents. The scheme provides subsidised housing for eligible families, including expectant parents, while awaiting completion of their new HDB flat. Consequently, the Ministry will not include an expected child past 24 weeks of gestational age in the PCI calculation for determining rental rates under this specific scheme.

results.title: Intended Carbon Tax Rate beyond 2027
results.summary: The Minister confirmed the Government's plan to progressively increase the carbon tax to $45 per tonne in 2026 and 2027, aiming to reach $50 to $80 per tonne by 2030. While the 2030 target remains stated, the Government retains the flexibility to review the tax trajectory periodically based on prevailing circumstances. No specific update on the rate beyond 2027 was provided at this time, with further details to be released in due course. The current trajectory is contingent on future assessments rather than a fixed schedule for post-2027 rates.

results.title: Internal Probe into PUB's Contract Irregularities Reported in AGO's FY2024/2025 Audit and Interim Governance Measures Implemented
results.summary: The Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment confirmed that the Public Utilities Board (PUB) has completed internal investigations into contract irregularities highlighted in the Auditor-General's Office (AGO) report, involving $148,900 in variation works and falsified biocide supply documents. While one case involved document alteration to cover incompleteness rather than financial loss, PUB reported a matter to the Police regarding potential fraud in variation works and issued a formal reprimand with a performance grade downgrade to the officer responsible for falsification. To strengthen governance, PUB has increased internal audit scopes, implemented digital tools including artificial intelligence for fraud detection, and tightened standard operating procedures to reduce human error. The agency assured that these measures will not compromise project speed or water security, while investigations into contractor integrity continue pending police outcomes.

results.title: Internal Review of AGO's FY2024/2025 Findings of Falsified Documents in PUB Contract
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the inquiry into falsified documents flagged in PUB's FY2024/2025 Auditor-General's Report was addressed in a previous response on 22 September 2025. Consequently, no new findings or specific preventive measures were provided in this session. The matter was deemed redundant as the comprehensive reply was already delivered at the earlier parliamentary sitting. Users seeking details on the investigation outcomes or PUB's updated prevention protocols should refer to the record of the 22 September 2025 debate.

results.title: Joint Environmental Impact Assessment in View of Close Proximity of Pulau Tekong and Changi Reclamation Projects
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) for the Changi reclamation projects have incorporated cumulative impacts from ongoing Pulau Tekong reclamation works. Specifically, the EIA for Changi North evaluated combined effects from both Changi Bay and Pulau Tekong projects, concluding that the overall environmental impact is negligible to minor. Consequently, a separate joint environmental impact assessment on the overall marine ecosystem has not been carried out, as existing assessments already address the cumulative risks. The Ministry will not implement a new joint assessment under current conditions.

results.title: Low-interest Financing Schemes for SMEs Undertaking Digital Transformation
results.summary: Enterprise Singapore administers the Enterprise Financing Scheme (EFS), which provides loans supporting SMEs' digital transformation plans. Under this scheme, the Government shares the risk for loan defaults to catalyse lending from participating financial institutions. Beyond EFS loans, the Government has implemented additional initiatives to support digital transformation journeys, including the SMEs Go Digital Programme and the Chief Technology Officer-as-a-Service. These measures aim to alleviate financial pressures and ensure businesses progressing in digitalisation are not placed in unsustainable positions.

results.title: Maintenance Regime of LRT and MRT Lines That Experienced Recent Disruptions
results.summary: Questions regarding long-term engineering upgrades for LRT and MRT point machines, current maintenance regimes for the East-West Line, and causes of specific faults on the North-South Line and Sengkang-Punggol LRT were raised. The Acting Minister for Transport noted that these inquiries have been addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24 at the 22 September 2025 Parliament Sitting. Specific details on upgrade timelines, inspection frequencies, fault causes, and remedial steps were not provided in this transcript. Commuters impacted by disruptions and aggregate duration statistics were also not stated. Further information is available through the referenced parliamentary record.

results.title: Measures to Address Bullying's Root Causes and Ensure Children Develop Self-protection Skills
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that a combined reply regarding upstream measures against bullying, the review of the Character and Citizenship Education curriculum, and strategies for fostering student assertiveness will be provided at the next available opportunity. This response will address questions on distinguishing aggression from legitimate retaliation, teaching self-protection skills, and fostering a civic society alongside other related Parliamentary Questions filed on this topic. No specific timelines, statistics, or immediate policy changes were announced in this session.

results.title: Median Waiting Time for Appointments at IMH Made through Different Channels
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the current median waiting time for appointments at the Institute of Mental Health (IMH) is 25 days. This figure applies uniformly across all appointment channels, including telephone, email, Health Hub self-service, the IMH Online Appointment Form, and referrals from general practitioners or polyclinics. No distinction in waiting times was reported between these specific access methods.

results.title: Medical Leave Entitlement for Attending Vaping Rehabilitation Sessions
results.summary: Under the Employment Act, workers attending vaping rehabilitation sessions are not eligible for paid sick leave as no medical certificates are issued for these sessions. The Minister clarified that only employees with a medical practitioner's certificate qualify for sick leave. Consequently, employers and employees must mutually agree on alternative leave arrangements, such as utilizing annual leave or taking no-pay leave, to allow attendance at rehabilitation sessions. No specific guidance document was mentioned for handling these cases beyond the requirement for mutual agreement.

results.title: Number and Causes of HDB Flat Fires that Resulted in Deaths and Injuries
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, the top three causes of HDB flat fires resulting in deaths or injuries were unattended cooking, electrical faults, and unattended lighted materials like candles or cigarettes. The Singapore Civil Defence Force does not track or report statistics distinguishing fatalities and injuries occurring inside versus outside the flat of origin. Consequently, no data exists to determine the proportion of such incidents affecting persons outside the fire's source location.

results.title: Number and Causes of Refuse Chute Fires
results.summary: Refuse chute fires are classified as minor incidents distinct from residential fires, typically caused by indiscriminate disposal of lighted materials like un-extinguished cigarette butts. The Singapore Civil Defence Force responded to an average of 54, 46, and 47 such fires per month in 2022, 2023, and 2024 respectively. These events are tracked separately due to their low risk and limited potential for fire spread. The Minister noted that comparing these statistics with residential fire data would not be meaningful.

results.title: Number of Applications and Approval Rates for Lift Access Housing Grant
results.summary: Since the Lift Access Housing Grant (LHG) launched in 2020, the Housing and Development Board has received 78 complete applications, with 60 approved. Of the approved cases, 12 beneficiaries utilized the grant increase from $30,000 to $80,000 effective April 2025. The LHG assists HDB households with medical or mobility challenges relocating to flats with direct lift access. The Minister provided these specific figures regarding application volumes, approval counts, and the impact of the recent grant hike.

results.title: Number of Approvals and Reports of Abuse Related to Training Employment Pass
results.summary: From 2021 to 2024, Training Employment Pass (TEP) approvals rose from 900 to 16,500 following post-pandemic border reopening, while abuse reports fluctuated between 9 and 200 annually. The Ministry of Manpower issued advisories to educate employers and intensified checks on applications to curb misuse. Enforcement operations identified approximately 70 companies under investigation for offences including false declarations in work pass applications. These measures aim to address the increasing volume of abuse reports linked to the scheme's expansion.

results.title: Number of Hawkers Who Have Hired LTVP or LTVP+ Holders to Work as Stall Assistants
results.summary: Starting 1 January 2025, the National Environment Agency (NEA) relaxed rules to allow hawker stallholders to hire Long Term Visit Pass (LTVP) or LTVP+ holders with Letters of Consent as stall assistants, removing the previous familial tie requirement. In the first six months of this revised policy, approximately 170 such holders were hired across over 120 hawker centres. The NEA will continue monitoring these figures to assess supply and demand dynamics. The Minister did not provide specific data on whether the current numbers indicate a limited supply pool or limited demand.

results.title: Number of IVF Embryos Discarded Due to Couples Divorcing or Death of Spouse
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track the number of IVF embryos discarded due to divorce or the death of a spouse. Instead, when assisted reproduction centres are informed of these circumstances, embryos are donated or discarded strictly in accordance with the couple's written instructions obtained prior to storage. This policy ensures that disposal actions reflect the specific directives provided by the individuals involved rather than relying on aggregated statistical data for these specific events.

results.title: Number of Migrant Domestic Workers with Caregiving Responsibilities at Secondary Residential Address
results.summary: As of August 2025, approximately 4,300 Migrant Domestic Workers (about 1.4% of the total population) are authorised to undertake caregiving responsibilities at a secondary residential address. Employers must notify the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and ensure the worker does not perform a full housework load in both households. This arrangement applies only to care for the employer or their household members, not general domestic duties. The Minister clarified that MDWs should primarily work at their declared residential address, with secondary address permissions subject to specific operational constraints.

results.title: Number of Vape-related Arrests Made, and Update on Enforcement, Preventive and Rehabilitative Measures for Tackling Vaping
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health announced a whole-of-Government response launched on 28 August 2025 to curb vaping, citing that over half of users are under 25 and 70 etomidate cases were detected this year. Key measures include removing over 2,800 online advertisements, enhancing border enforcement with agencies like HSA and ICA, and empowering multiple ministries including MHA and MOH to act against etomidate offences. The Ministry confirmed that 6,000 vapes were surrendered via the "Bin The Vape" programme and 74 individuals sought voluntary help. While new legislation to close legal loopholes is expected early next year, current enforcement relies on existing Class C drug classifications for etomidate. Regional collaboration is ongoing, with several ASEAN nations already banning vaping.

results.title: Number, Profile and Success Rate of Applications for SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower confirmed that a related question regarding the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support Scheme, filed by Ms Elysa Chen on 23 September 2025, will be addressed jointly with the current inquiry. The response will cover the total number of applications received, the breakdown of successful versus unsuccessful applicants by age group (below and above 40), and the top three reasons for rejection. Specific statistics and operational details regarding the scheme's success rates and eligibility constraints are not yet available in this transcript.

results.title: Percentage of In-service Early Childhood Educators Who Have Completed Certificate in Inclusive Practice Course
results.summary: The National Institute of Early Childhood Development (NIEC) launched the Certificate in Inclusive Practice (CIP) in 2022, recording an annual intake of approximately 80 in-service educators. Concurrently, the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) collaborates with training providers to embed inclusive practice modules into pre-service and continuing professional development curricula, focusing on competencies like behaviour guidance and differentiated instruction. Additionally, ECDA piloted the Inclusive Support Programme (InSP) across six preschools to integrate early intervention services for children requiring medium-level support. Participants in this pilot undergo specific training and coaching to enhance their ability to assist children with additional needs within the preschool environment.

results.title: Post-incident Support for Scam Victims beyond Public Education Workshops and Fund Recovery Efforts
results.summary: The Police have launched a multilingual resource guide detailing post-incident support for scam victims, which is being progressively distributed to all households and available online via the Singapore Police Force and ScamShield websites. Additionally, the Victim Care Cadre Programme assigns volunteer Victim Care Officers to provide psychological first aid and crisis intervention upon request or in specific cases. These officers also direct victims to community and professional resources for longer-term psychological and financial assistance, complementing existing fund recovery efforts and public education workshops.

results.title: Proceedings on Ministerial Statements
results.summary: A business motion proposes taking three Ministerial Statements in a specific order: first, the Minister for Foreign Affairs' statement on the Middle East situation; second, a related statement by the Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs; and third, the Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs' statement on Singaporeans' response. This sequence precedes the debate on the Address of Thanks and the business motion on Speech Time. The Leader of the Opposition's statement on duties and privileges is also scheduled for this sitting to establish the framework for the term. The Foreign Affairs statements are prioritized to allow the public to hear the Government's position before Minister Vivian Balakrishnan departs for the 80th United Nations General Assembly tonight. Members will have sufficient time to seek clarifications on these statements before the Address of Thanks debate commences.

results.title: Process in Engaging Stakeholders on Reclamation Projects with Potential Ecological Consequences
results.summary: Under Singapore's Environment Impact Assessment (EIA) framework, reclamation projects with potential ecological impacts must undergo detailed environmental studies to assess effects on coastlines and marine environments. Developing agencies engage stakeholders, including nature groups, academics, researchers, and the interested public, for feedback on study scope, findings, and mitigation measures once the EIA is publicly disclosed. These engagements occur early in the planning process to incorporate inputs into development plans before implementation. The framework ensures surrounding habitats are considered, and public feedback is formally shared with agencies during the disclosure phase.

results.title: Profile of Applicants under the Family Scheme Category of Public Rental Scheme from 2019 to 2024
results.summary: From 2019 to 2024, the Housing and Development Board reviewed Public Rental Scheme Family Scheme applications holistically, considering individual circumstances and social worker input. Annual average proportions of requests from single unmarried parents, non-citizen single parents with Singaporean children, and divorced parents were 7%, 4%, and 17% respectively, noting some group overlap. During this period, approximately 4,000 unique applicants across these categories were successful. The Minister did not provide specific breakdowns of successful applicants by individual group, only the aggregate figure.

results.title: Profile of Families Benefiting from Concessionary Migrant Domestic Worker Levy
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, an average of 100,000 employers annually benefited from a reduced Migrant Domestic Worker levy of $60 per month (down from $300) for households with a Singapore Citizen child under 16. This concession applies to all eligible households regardless of parental status, as the Ministry of Manpower does not track first-time parent proportions. Households hiring domestic workers in the months leading up to childbirth remain eligible for the concession once the child is born. Application processes and timelines for work permits are available online to assist families in planning for newborn arrivals.

results.title: Progressively Reducing Frequency of Flights from Paya Lebar Airbase Leading Up to Its Decommissioning in 2030
results.summary: The Ministry confirmed that the relocation of Paya Lebar Airbase (PLAB) remains on track for 2030 or beyond, with flight frequency reductions planned progressively as infrastructure readiness dictates. To mitigate noise impacts on residents in Potong Pasir and Bidadari, the RSAF has implemented specific measures including conducting approximately 50% of flying training overseas, utilizing simulators for local requirements, and adjusting training hours during school examinations. Additionally, less than 1% of flights occur between 10.00 pm and 8.00 am unless for operational reasons, while routes are planned to avoid residential areas where possible. These actions collectively aim to safeguard operational readiness while managing community impact during the decommissioning phase.

results.title: Proportion of HDB Flat Fires Linked to Electrical Causes that Occurred in Pre-1997 and Post-1997 Flats
results.summary: In 2024, 218 HDB fires were attributed to electrical faults, with 129 (59.2%) occurring in flats built up to 1997. Among these older flats, 93 fires (42.7%) involved units undergoing or awaiting Home Improvement Programme works, while 36 (16.5%) involved other upgrades. The remaining 89 fires (40.8%) occurred in flats built after 1997. The Singapore Civil Defence Force does not track whether electrical fault fires are specifically caused by faulty or old wiring, so no specific count for this sub-category is available.

results.title: Proportion of Singapore Citizens and Foreign Spouses-to-be Who Have Completed Pre-Marriage LTVP Assessment
results.summary: In 2024, 64% of foreign spouses of Singapore citizens applying for a Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP) completed the Pre-Marriage LTVP Assessment (PMLA) before application. The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) raised awareness through multilingual advisories on its website, search engine optimizations, and the "Our Marriage Journey" portal. Additionally, officers at the Registry of Marriages and Registry of Muslim Marriages actively prompt couples to complete the assessment prior to applying. These efforts aim to encourage more couples to undertake the PMLA before seeking LTVP approval.

results.title: Proposal for Set-up of Smaller Satellite HDB Service Office in Hougang
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) closed its Hougang Branch office at Block 814 Hougang Central on 1 September 2025 to facilitate mixed commercial and residential development at Hougang Ave 10 and Hougang Central. To maintain service continuity, HDB opened a 24-hour e-Lobby at Block 806 Hougang Central equipped with self-help machines for transactions like mortgage and rental payments, alongside digital options via HDB InfoWEB and Mobile@HDB app. As a transitional measure, a service ambassador stationed at the e-Lobby will guide residents until October 2025, with potential extension based on demand. Residents requiring wet-ink signatures or complex assistance can book appointments at the Sengkang Branch or request in-person service at the Hougang e-Lobby for those with accessibility needs or exceptional circumstances.

results.title: Proposal to Allow Singles Especially Widows and Divorcees Aged 50 and Above to Apply for 3-room BTO HDB Flats
results.summary: The Minister clarified that widowed or divorced first-timer citizens with care and control of at least one child may purchase new or resale flats of any size, subject to prevailing eligibility conditions. Under the Assistance Scheme for Second-Timers (Divorced/Widowed Parents), applicants with a child aged 18 and below receive priority for 2-room Flexi or 3-room BTO flats in Standard projects. All other eligible first-timer singles aged 35 and above, including those without children, can buy a new 2-room Flexi flat island-wide. The Ministry noted these measures balance housing needs against land constraints.

results.title: Proposal to Restrict Smoking at or Near Windows or Balconies of HDB or Private Residential Units
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated there are currently no plans to extend smoking restrictions to HDB or private apartments, specifically prohibiting smoking at or near windows and balconies. Consequently, the proposed measure to restrict smoking in these residential areas will not be implemented. The existing enforcement mechanisms for high-rise littering will not be applied to smoking in these locations as no new restrictions are being introduced. This confirms the status quo regarding smoking policies in private and public housing units remains unchanged.

results.title: Proposal to Use Excess U-Save Rebates to Offset Service and Conservancy Charges
results.summary: The Minister clarified that U-Save rebates are strictly intended to offset utility expenses and possess no expiry date, allowing unused amounts to be rolled forward for future utility bills. Consequently, households cannot use excess U-Save rebates to offset Service and Conservancy Charges (S&CC). Instead, eligible households will receive a separate Goods & Services Tax Voucher – S&CC Rebate in Financial Year 2025, which offsets between 1.5 and 3.5 months of S&CC. This measure maintains the distinct allocation of funds for utilities versus service charges.

results.title: Provision of and Subsidy for Personal Tracking Devices for Caregivers of Children with Autism under Assistive Technology Fund
results.summary: The Assistive Technology Fund supports personal tracking devices like Apple AirTags for autistic children to promote independent living, though no applications have been received to date. Eligible persons with disabilities may purchase these devices for early intervention, education, or therapy. While caregivers can choose specific tracking tools based on preference, the Ministry will not proactively provide starter devices upon diagnosis via early-intervention centres. The decision to utilise such devices rests entirely with the caregivers, meaning no automatic allocation is planned.

results.title: Quantifying Potential Cost Impact of New Fixed Installation Plan Submission Requirement on Lift Modernisation Projects
results.summary: Starting 1 October 2025, a new requirement mandates fixed installation plan submissions by qualified professionals for lift modernisation projects. The Building and Construction Authority estimates this will incur additional costs of 0.5% to 2% of total installation expenses, roughly $500 to $2,000. To mitigate impact, the Authority will allow a single application submission for identical lift designs or modifications across multiple locations. The Minister did not confirm if a formal cost impact study has been commissioned or specify further support measures for Town Councils beyond the streamlined application process.

results.title: Reason for Engine Capacity, Maximum Power and Open Market Value Thresholds in Qualifying for COE Premium and ARF Waiver under Disabled Persons Scheme
results.summary: The Disabled Persons Scheme exempts eligible persons with disabilities unable to use public transport from COE premiums and ARF for work-related commuting, provided their vehicle has an engine capacity of 1,600cc or less, maximum power of 97kW, and an open market value not exceeding $20,000. These thresholds aim to ensure affordability and fiscal prudence. The Minister stated that appeals regarding these criteria can be considered on a case-by-case basis, particularly where a disability justifies a larger-capacity or higher-value vehicle. No specific appeal process or timeline was detailed in the response.

results.title: Reasons for Non-detection of Overstayer who Surrendered Passport in 1996 and Measures to Prevent Similar Incidents
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs addressed concerns regarding Ms. Yu Zefang, an overstayer who surrendered her passport in 1996 but remained undetected for over 28 years. The Minister explained that Ms. Yu deliberately maintained a low profile by avoiding fixed accommodation, formal employment, local bank accounts, and government services like public healthcare, which prevented identity authentication. Consequently, these specific vulnerabilities allowed her to evade regular ICA ground operations and enforcement actions. Regarding her daughter's Permanent Residence application, the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority is currently investigating the circumstances to determine if fraud or harbouring offences occurred. The agency will take appropriate action if such offences are disclosed and will continue to improve measures to detect and arrest overstayers.

results.title: Reducing Civil Service Job Postings That Focus on Academic Requirements to Emphasise Skills-based Hiring
results.summary: The Public Service Division supports skills-based hiring, with most job advertisements excluding specific academic requirements. Agencies evaluate candidates holistically based on skills, competencies, work experience, and job alignment. However, roles in specialised or technical fields like accountancy, engineering, and legal services may still mandate relevant professional qualifications. In these instances, agencies will explain the necessity of such requirements to ensure effective job performance.

results.title: Replenishing Reserves Drawn During COVID-19 Crisis and Impact on Taxes Imposed on Singaporeans
results.summary: Between FY2020 and FY2022, Singapore drew approximately $40 billion from Past Reserves to support COVID-19 measures. The Minister for Finance stated that replenishing these drawn reserves is not expected, as future spending increases will require deploying fiscal resources to maintain a balanced budget over the medium term. Consequently, the government will not restore the specific amount drawn during the crisis. While surpluses accumulated at the end of a Government term will remain protected as Past Reserves, the query regarding a timeframe for replenishment and potential tax increases or reduced public services does not arise for answer.

results.title: Reports of Workplace Harassment and Bullying Cases, and Actions against Companies with Repeated Reports
results.summary: Between January 2021 and July 2025, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) received an average of 57 workplace harassment complaints annually. For complaints requiring employer follow-up, TAFEP mandates independent investigations and interim safeguards such as no-contact orders, remote work, or re-deployment if the affected party's well-being is at risk. TAFEP assesses past complaints against a company to determine appropriate actions and requires updates on investigation outcomes, disciplinary measures, and the implementation of prevention policies aligned with the Tripartite Advisory. The agency partners with community organisations to expand referral channels but does not track cases handled through other reporting channels like the Workplace Safety and Health Incident Reporting eService or State Courts. TAFEP reports egregious companies to the Ministry for Manpower that fail to provide a safe environment or refuse corrective action.

results.title: Retirement of Clerk of Parliament
results.summary: Ms Ng Sheau Jiuan, Clerk of Parliament since 2005, retires after 33 years of service, having overseen key transformations including livestreaming proceedings, simplifying parliamentary language, and improving Members' Room facilities. Her tenure covered six terms of Parliament, five Speakers, and an increasing workload involving Parliamentary Questions and Motions. Mr Siow Peng Han is appointed as the new Clerk of Parliament effective tomorrow, succeeding Ms Ng. Mr Siow has served as Deputy Clerk since 2008 and will lead the Parliamentary Service into the 15th Parliament. The transition ensures continuity in managing parliamentary procedures, Standing Orders, and public outreach initiatives aimed at making proceedings more accessible. Both officials bring extensive legal training and experience to their respective roles within the Parliamentary Service.

results.title: Review of Animals and Birds Act, and Recommendations in White Paper on Strengthening Protections for Animal Welfare
results.summary: The National Parks Board (NParks) is conducting a comprehensive review of the Animals and Birds Act 1965 to update penalty frameworks, including imprisonment terms, fines, and disqualification orders, alongside enforcement powers. While some recommendations from the jointly submitted 2025 White Paper by ACRES and SPCA regarding online content prevention and enhanced group collaboration will not be included in this specific review, other proposals may be adopted or further evaluated with partners. NParks aims to commence public consultation on the draft Bill by the end of 2026, following engagements with stakeholders in July. The review focuses on deterring cruelty and reducing disamenities from irresponsible pet ownership through initiatives like cat licensing and pet ownership courses, with firm enforcement actions taken based on offence severity and repeat offender status.

results.title: Review of Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act 2004, and Addressing Maintenance Issues in Ageing Condominium Developments
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority is reviewing the Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act 2004 to strengthen self-governance and support effective estate maintenance. The review specifically addresses ageing infrastructure issues in older condominiums and inadequate sinking funds required for maintenance or upgrades. This legislative review is scheduled for completion by the first half of next year. The response covers similar questions raised by other Members regarding these maintenance challenges.

results.title: Review of Legislation and Regulations to Mandate Organisations to Store Video and CCTV Footage
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information stated that under the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA), organisations must implement policies to preserve personal data during access requests under section 21. Existing safeguards include data retention requirements for reviewing denied requests and criminal penalties for intentional concealment or destruction of records. The Ministry regularly reviews the PDPA framework to ensure alignment with national objectives. The Minister did not confirm whether the Personal Data Protection Commission will mandate specific storage periods for CCTV footage beyond current preservation obligations.

results.title: Review of Penalty Framework for Animal Cruelty and Abuse Offences
results.summary: The National Parks Board (NParks) is conducting a comprehensive review of the Animals and Birds Act 1965 to enhance penalties, including imprisonment terms, fines, and disqualification orders, alongside stronger enforcement powers. The review explicitly excludes proposals to ban online animal abuse content or increase collaboration with animal welfare groups. NParks aims to commence public consultation on the draft Bill by around end-2026, following stakeholder engagements with groups like the Animal Concerns Research and Education Society in July. Current enforcement actions consider offence severity and repeat offender status, while initiatives like cat licensing and the Pet Ownership Course address irresponsible pet ownership. Recent investigations into community cat incidents in Yishun and Punggol concluded they were likely vehicular accidents, prompting firm enforcement where appropriate.

results.title: Reviewing Merchant Eligibility Criteria of CDC Voucher Scheme to Ensure Fairness for Small Businesses
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that the Community Development Council (CDC) Voucher Scheme, launched in June 2020, primarily supports hawkers and heartland merchants in HDB areas, with selective onboarding of small businesses in locations lacking traditional merchants. While the 2023 expansion allowed half of the 2024 tranche vouchers to be used at supermarkets, the remaining half remains restricted to heartland merchants to balance resident needs with local business support. The scheme has onboarded over 23,000 merchants, achieving a 97% spend rate in 2024 and receiving 98% resident satisfaction regarding merchant availability in January 2025. The Government commits to ongoing monitoring and reviews with partners like the Federation of Merchants' Association to ensure the scheme's effectiveness and fairness for small businesses.

results.title: Reviewing Regulation of Integrated Shield Plans to Align with National Healthcare Priorities and Patient Protection
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health acknowledged concerns regarding escalating private hospital bills, rising premiums, and stricter claims safeguards under Integrated Shield Plans. He stated that the questions concerning disallowing "as-charged" coverage, sufficiency of anti-profitereing laws, and regulation of panel specialists address a deeper systemic issue. The Minister indicated he would address these specific queries in detail at the subsequent Sitting alongside other similar Parliamentary Questions. No immediate policy changes, timelines, or definitive regulatory constraints were announced during this session. The current regulatory framework remains unchanged pending further discussion in the next parliamentary sitting.
