results.title: Reviewing Safety Protocols for Overseas School Trips
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) regularly reviews safety protocols for all MOE schools, ensuring they apply to overseas trips. Non-MOE schools retain autonomy over their own policies, operations, and governance, including safety protocols, allowing them to tailor measures to their specific circumstances and activities. The Minister clarified that while MOE protocols are standardised for government schools, private institutions are not subject to a mandatory standardised framework but must manage their own safety standards independently. No new review timeline or specific statutory changes were announced regarding the fatal accident incident.

results.title: Safety Measures at Self-use Facilities in SAF Camps Such As Swimming Pools and Fitness Centres
results.summary: Medically and physically fit Singapore Armed Forces personnel may use self-initiated fitness activities on SAF premises, provided they take precautions like carrying mobile phones and pairing with a buddy for off-site runs. Gyms and swimming pools are manned by duty personnel or lifeguards during operating hours, while off-hours use requires adherence to self-initiated activity protocols. Since late August 2025, added restrictions prohibit swimming alone when no lifeguards are on site. Personnel must contact emergency services for medical issues and use assessment forms to verify suitability before engaging in self-initiated activities. These measures aim to mitigate risks associated with self-use facilities within SAF camps.

results.title: Significance of Closure of ASEAN Engagement Office by US Government
results.summary: The Ministry assesses the US closure of its ASEAN Engagement Office as an internal reorganisation that does not alter the longstanding, substantive partnership between ASEAN and the US, which supports over 625,000 American jobs and holds a $798.8 billion FDI stock in 2023. Singapore maintains deep, bipartisan relations with the US spanning defence, security, and emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing, with ongoing preliminary tariff negotiations to facilitate pharmaceutical and semiconductor exports. The government continues to engage the Trump administration and cultivate support in the US Congress, including a recent resolution introduced by the Singapore Caucus Co-chairs to express continued bilateral support. Future cooperation will expand to include private sector players in tackling cyber scams and operationalising the ASEAN-US Responsible AI Roadmap to ensure the region remains a secure destination for US business.

results.title: Singapore's Support in Humanitarian Aid for Critical Food Security Situation in Gaza Conflict and Review on Stance on Palestinian Statehood
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs will deliver a Ministerial Statement later regarding Singapore's position on the Gaza conflict, addressing multiple Parliamentary Questions filed by MPs. The response will cover Singapore's humanitarian assistance plans for children facing food insecurity, diplomatic efforts to ensure safe delivery of supplies, and specific messages conveyed to Israel concerning civilian protection and essential services. The Minister will also outline the Government's assessment of ceasefire prospects, potential increases in funds for post-conflict rebuilding, and measures to ensure Israel adheres to international law amidst regional tensions. Additionally, the statement will address whether Singapore will convene an inter-ministry taskforce to consolidate relief efforts and initiatives to safeguard domestic community harmony against the backdrop of the crisis.

results.title: Speech Time for the Leader of the Opposition
results.summary: Effective immediately and until the end of the 15th Parliament, Standing Order 48(8) is suspended to grant the Leader of the Opposition, Mr Pritam Singh, a speaking duration of up to 40 minutes on any parliamentary question. This measure replaces the standard time limits previously applicable to opposition leaders. The extension applies specifically to Mr Singh's tenure as Leader of the Opposition for the current parliamentary term.

results.title: Strategies for Improving Employment Outcomes for Youths and Proportion of Graduating Students Taking Up Contract Roles
results.summary: Singapore's NEET youth rate remains low at 4.1% (16,900 individuals) in 2024, significantly below peers like Sweden (5.5%) and the UK (13%), reflecting strong engagement in education and the labour market. Re-engagement strategies include Education and Career Guidance (ECG) by counsellors, career matching via Workforce Singapore (WSG), reskilling services with e2i, and the Mentoring SG movement for complex cases. Social and financial support is available through Family Service Centres and Social Service Offices. Specific statistics on the proportion of graduating students taking contract roles versus permanent employment were not provided in this response; the Minister directed the Member to a separate reply scheduled for 23 September regarding graduate employment and the GRaduate Industry Traineeships programme.

results.title: Strengthening Protection for Singapore’s Critical Information Infrastructure Given Rise in Advanced Persistent Threat Attacks
results.summary: Suspected Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) attacks on Singapore increased more than fourfold from 2021 to 2024, with recent detections involving actor UNC3886 targeting critical information infrastructure (CII). Under the Cybersecurity Act and Code of Practice, CII owners must meet baseline requirements aligned with international standards like IEC 62443 and MITRE ATT&CK, conduct regular cybersecurity testing and audits, and safeguard systems against known vulnerabilities. The Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA) collaborates with domestic and international partners, including ASEAN Regional Computer Emergency Response Team, through CERT-to-CERT exchanges and joint cross-border operations to strengthen deterrence. To raise awareness, CSA conducts the annual Exercise Cyber Star nationwide exercise to test critical sectors' readiness for cyber crises. CSA is currently reviewing how to better protect CII with relevant agencies and stakeholders, with further details to be shared when ready.

results.title: Structuring Carbon Relief Allowances to Support Emissions-intensive Trade-exposed Companies and Retain Jobs for Singaporeans
results.summary: The Ministry adopts a judicious approach to structuring carbon relief allowances under the transition framework to support emissions-intensive trade-exposed (EITE) companies. This strategy aims to facilitate an orderly transition, ensuring these firms remain in Singapore to retain local jobs and maintain value-add within integrated supply chains. The government prioritizes preventing company relocation to jurisdictions with lower carbon prices, recognizing that their exit would negatively impact local suppliers and service providers. The commitment focuses on helping EITE sectors continue economic activities while adding value to the economy.

results.title: Success Rates of Assisted Conception Procedures at Public Hospitals
results.summary: The Minister for Health provided Assisted Reproduction Technology (ART) cycle data for public hospitals covering 2021 to 2023. The response details the number of cases exceeding co-funded scheme cycle limits alongside their success rates, as well as the count of successful cycles within the limits. Specific numerical figures for these metrics are contained in Table 1 referenced during the parliamentary exchange. No further analysis or projections regarding future trends were included in the stated response.

results.title: Take-up Rate for Hawker Succession Scheme and Review of Scheme to Discourage Subletting of Hawker Stalls
results.summary: Between 2012 and 2024, the National Environment Agency (NEA) issued approximately 230 termination notices for stallholders failing to personally operate their stalls, with over 230 warnings and 30 terminations recorded in 2024 alone. The Hawker Succession Scheme (HSS), launched on 1 January 2022, aims to facilitate succession rather than address subletting by matching retiring hawkers with aspiring successors for apprenticeship and transfer. As of the current reporting period, six veteran hawkers have successfully completed transfers via the HSS, four are in the process, and NEA is assisting two others to find successors. The Minister clarified that the scheme's objective is not to discourage subletting but to support skill and recipe transfer within families or to relatives.

results.title: Situation in the Middle East
results.summary: Singapore reaffirmed its foreign policy position on the Israel-Gaza conflict, stating it will recognize the State of Palestine only upon the establishment of an effective government that accepts Israel's right to exist and renounces terrorism. The government opposes unilateral annexation of occupied territories as a breach of international law and supports a negotiated two-state solution as the sole viable pathway to lasting peace. Singapore condemned the October 7 Hamas attacks and the killing of civilians, while calling for the immediate, unconditional release of all hostages. It pledged to continue calling out actions by all parties that violate international law, noting it will reconsider its recognition stance if the situation deteriorates or if Israel undermines the two-state solution. Singapore also emphasized the need for humanitarian aid access and condemned the deliberate denial of such aid.

results.title: Timely Replacement of Faulty Equipment in Public Playgrounds
results.summary: Town Councils manage and maintain HDB playgrounds, conducting periodic checks to ensure safety per Singapore Standards SS457. Defective equipment must be rectified expeditiously based on prevailing industry standards. Maintenance contracts typically include clauses stipulating repair or replacement requirements. The Ministry does not directly mandate replacement timelines but relies on Town Councils to enforce these standards through existing contractual provisions. No new procurement clauses were announced for immediate inclusion in vendor contracts.

results.title: Total Annual Imputed Rental Value of Properties Allocated to Anchor Operators and Partner Operators of Preschool Services
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Anchor Operator preschool centres allocated Housing Development Board premises are charged rental rates comparable to other socio-communal facilities, rather than receiving market-rate exemptions. Consequently, no total estimated annual imputed rental value exists for these properties as they are not subsidized below market rates. Furthermore, the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) currently does not allocate any sites to Partner Operator centres. Therefore, the Ministry does not factor any in-kind rental subsidy into financial efficiency evaluations for Partner Operators, as no such allocations occur.

results.title: Tracking Tenure Duration and Ethnicity of Tenants for Open-market Rental of HDB Flats
results.summary: Tenants renting HDB flats on the open market are not subject to the Ethnic Integration Policy (EIP), as their temporary stays typically do not alter long-term ethnic proportions in public housing estates. To prevent foreigner enclaves, HDB introduced a non-Citizen quota in 2014 that caps the number of flats wholly rented to non-Malaysian non-citizens within each block and neighbourhood. Eligible owners must fulfill the minimum occupation period, obtain HDB approval, and adhere to this quota. Additionally, rental periods for non-Malaysian non-citizen tenants are strictly capped at two years. If irregularities regarding these rules are detected, HDB will take appropriate enforcement action against the flat owner. The majority of open-market tenants are non-citizens from diverse ethnic backgrounds and countries.

results.title: Update on Evaluation of Inclusive Support Programme Pilot in Preschools
results.summary: The evaluation of the Inclusive Support Programme (InSP) pilot in preschools is nearing completion. The assessment measured impacts on the learning and social development of children with developmental needs, typically developing children, and their caregivers. Specific evaluation factors and the timeline for scaling up the programme to more preschools, should the pilot prove successful, will be announced soon by the Ministry for Social and Family Development. Further details regarding the programme's future expansion are pending official announcement.

results.title: Update on Lift Retrofitting Plans for Overhead Bridges in Fernvale and Extension of Programme to Sengkang West
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) will prioritise installing lifts at the pedestrian overhead bridge along Sengkang West Way near Block 496B, serving a larger senior population. Three additional bridges along Sengkang West Way will receive lifts in the next phase of the retrofitting programme. The remaining three Fernvale bridges are excluded from lift installation as they are located near at-grade crossings or already possess ramps, providing barrier-free access. This assessment covers both Fernvale and Sengkang West based on pedestrian traffic volumes.

results.title: Singaporeans' Response to Situation in the Middle East
results.summary: Singapore maintains that formal recognition of the State of Palestine is contingent upon the establishment of an effective Palestinian government that renounces terrorism and recognizes Israel, shifting its stance from a previous "prepared in principle" position to this specific conditionality. While Singapore supports a two-state solution aligned with UN Resolutions 242 and 2334, it currently views Hamas's control as a barrier to meeting these prerequisites, thereby delaying immediate recognition despite ongoing diplomatic efforts. The government emphasizes that tactical military victories by Israel could undermine long-term strategic peace, rejecting the "from the river to the sea" slogan for both parties. Consequently, Singapore continues to leverage its diplomatic channels and humanitarian aid to facilitate dialogue without imposing broader sanctions on Israel, reserving such measures for situations where international law is severely violated or the humanitarian situation in Gaza deteriorates further.

results.title: Update on Review to Remove 15-month Wait-out Requirement for Private Property Owners Downgrading to HDB Flats
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the 15-month wait-out requirement for private property owners downgrading to non-subsidised HDB flats remains in place as part of September 2022 cooling measures. Citizens aged 55 and above are exempt from this period when moving to a 4-room or smaller resale flat to support senior retirement needs. Since 2022, HDB has processed approximately 1,800 annual appeals for waivers, acceding to about one in four cases involving financial difficulties or extenuating circumstances where no alternative housing exists. The agency does not track appeals by the specific flat type sought. Officials plan to remove the wait-out period when market conditions allow, with related questions scheduled for subsequent Sittings.

results.title: Use of AI and Technology Tools to Reduce Errors and Increase Efficiency in Government Contract Evaluation, Award and Management
results.summary: The Government is actively exploring and has begun deploying artificial intelligence and other technological measures within suitable procurement areas to reduce errors and increase efficiency in tender evaluation and contract management. This initiative aims to ensure contracts are awarded in alignment with the Government's procurement principles, addressing findings from the Auditor-General's Office regarding FY24/25 contract management. Specific AI tools, implementation timelines, or detailed eligibility criteria for these technologies were not explicitly stated in the response.

results.title: Use of Facial Recognition Technology to Identify and Stop Perpetrators of Petty Crimes
results.summary: The Police may deploy facial recognition technology to identify perpetrators of petty crimes, such as bicycle theft, provided closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage is available at the crime scene. This capability allows for follow-up actions as appropriate once potential culprits are identified. The Minister clarified that the use of this technology is contingent upon the existence of existing CCTV infrastructure where the offenses occur. No specific impediments to accountability were detailed beyond the requirement for available surveillance footage.

results.title: Toward Certificate of Entitlement (COE) 2.0
results.summary: The Government maintains the Certificate of Entitlement (COE) system as necessary due to Singapore's land constraints, noting that a full car ownership model would require 1.5 million vehicles, exceeding the current population. While acknowledging concerns over rising COE prices driven by Private Hire Car companies and the need for equity for parents of multiple children, caregivers, and disabled persons, the administration rejects a pure needs-based allocation or complete repeal of the Vehicle Quota System. Instead, the current position favors a market allocation mechanism with potential targeted discounts to minimize short-term disamenities, utilizing COE revenues to subsidize public transport and fund healthcare and education. The proposal to create a separate COE category for Private Hire Cars or move them to Category E remains under discussion, with the Government prioritizing mass public transport to achieve the greatest good for the greatest number.

results.title: Utilisation Rate of Home Nursing Services and Plans to Expand Provision of Services
results.summary: As of June 2025, the utilisation rate for subsidised home nursing services in Singapore stands at approximately 80%. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that home nursing capacity is reviewed regularly to ensure adequate provision meets demand. Specific plans to expand services were not detailed in the response, as the Minister indicated that regular reviews are the mechanism used to maintain sufficient capacity rather than outlining a distinct expansion timeline or new programme.

results.title: President's Speech
results.summary: The President framed Singapore's current challenges as fundamental shifts requiring a "we first" society built on trust and resilience to navigate geopolitical rivalry, AI disruption, and resource constraints. To address rising costs and job security, the government emphasized that sustainable solutions depend on wages increasing faster than costs through higher productivity, while unions must shift focus from protecting jobs to safeguarding workers' futures. Specific measures include upskilling existing workers alongside new hires for high-growth industries, establishing national and sectoral AI centres of excellence, and developing the space industry via the Office for Space Technology and Industry for low-orbit communication. The government also committed to long-term climate adaptation strategies and confirmed the M³@Towns initiative as a joint effort, though specific details on certain opposition queries regarding tariff impacts and space agency formation were deferred to the Deputy Prime Minister.

results.title: Utilisation Rate of Parenting for Wellness Initiative Portal and Resources
results.summary: The Parenting for Wellness Initiative, launched in September 2024 by the Health Promotion Board, Ministry of Education, and Ministry of Social and Family Development, has reached 69,000 unique website visitors. Over 2,700 registered users have signed up for a personalised dashboard that recommends learning modules and tracks progress. Additionally, more than 76,000 copies of the Parenting for Wellness Toolbox have been distributed via digital downloads and physical copies at events including the Digital for Life Festival and National Family Festival. Early feedback indicates parents find the resources relevant and practical, while educators note the materials support their work with families. The government plans to continue refining platform resources to meet evolving parental needs.

results.title: Utilisation Rate of Take-A-Break Caregiver Respite Programme
results.summary: The Take-A-Break home-based respite care pilot, launched by SG Enable in 2020, has supported approximately 400 caregivers with over 20,000 hours of respite as of 31 August 2025. The Ministry intends to increase awareness through collaborations with disability service providers, hospitals, Enabling Services Hubs, and community organisations like CaringSG. For caregivers temporarily unable to provide care due to medical procedures or surgeries, available options include home-based services, enrolment in community facility activities, and short-term respite at disability homes. Further information on these respite care options is accessible via the Enabling Guide website. SG Enable continues to review the programme to better meet caregiver needs.

results.title: Ventilation and Cooling Standards in Schools to Ensure Equitable, Cognitively Optimal Learning Environment for All Students
results.summary: The Ministry of Education aims to ensure equitable, cognitively optimal learning environments by implementing heat-resilient school designs and active cooling measures. Specific actions include orienting new buildings north-south for natural ventilation, applying cool paint to exteriors, adding shade and greenery, and installing mixed-mode air-conditioning in all school halls. Existing classrooms will receive additional high-volume, low-speed fans, while new schools will feature more windows and open spaces. The Ministry sets air-conditioners at appropriate temperatures combined with these fans to achieve thermal comfort in an energy-efficient manner. Additionally, uniform guidelines have been reviewed to allow physical education attire on most days. The Ministry will continue exploring further heat adaptation measures based on local climate and scientific research, noting that specific hardware standards for all classrooms are still under exploration.

results.title: Waiting Times for Enrolment into Day Activity Centres
results.summary: As of August 2025, the mean and median waiting time for enrolment into Ministry of Social and Family Development-funded Day Activity Centres is approximately eight months, with an overall utilisation rate of about 90%. The Ministry plans to progressively increase capacity by 500 places by 2030. In the interim, persons with disabilities may access the Special Transition and Engagement Programme for graduates of Special Education schools or participate in activities via the Enabling Services Hub pilot. The projected waiting time for 2030 was not explicitly stated in the response.

results.title: Workplace Burnout Reported in Past Three Years and Measures to Promote Workplace Mental Well-being
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not systematically collect workplace burnout data, though anonymised iWorkHealth tool usage in 2024 indicates about one in three employees experienced work-related stress or burnout, a proportion stable since 2021. Participation remains voluntary, limiting full workforce representativeness. To promote mental well-being, MOM and the Workplace Safety and Health Council have implemented measures including the Tripartite Advisory on Mental Health, which guides employers on flexible work arrangements and after-hours communication policies. The Well-being Champions Network facilitates best practice exchange and resource access among participating firms. A 2023 survey showed over 90% of surveyed firms implemented at least one mental well-being measure. MOM continues collaborating with Tripartite Partners to monitor effectiveness and explore enhancements.

results.title: e2i’s Effectiveness in Supporting Retrenched Persons with Placement and Wage Matters
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, the Taskforce for Responsible Retrenchment and Employment Facilitation supported retrenched workers through career coaching by Workforce Singapore and NTUC/e2i. On average, about 70% of these workers were placed within six months, with a median time-to-placement of approximately three months. Among those placed within this period, nearly half secured positions offering wages similar to or higher than their previous roles. The Minister noted that this multi-agency effort has consistently provided effective support for employment facilitation over the past three years.
